rule: raw:design-strategy-strategy-exploration-omega-cycle (hive raw 47) + raw:design-strategy-falsifier-retire-rule (hive raw 71, report-only) + raw:design-strategy-honesty-admissibility (hive raw 73) + raw:design-strategy-audit-append-only (hive raw 77) + raw:design-strategy-sentinel-update (hive raw 80) + raw:design-strategy-ai-native-error-message (hive raw 66)
purpose: Propagate R1 cmd_sha256 + HIT/ERROR semantic disambiguation defenses from falsifier_health.sh to bridge_health.sh. Bridges face higher attack risk than falsifiers because they are invoked by external API selftest paths (network-reachable subversion surface). Establish per-bridge SHA256 baseline TSV + runtime mismatch → STATUS=TAMPERED defense.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 prompt: 'Ω-cycle (오메가 사이클) — propagate R1 cmd_sha256 + HIT/ERROR semantics from falsifier_health.sh to bridge_health.sh'

inputs.donor: tool/falsifier_health.sh (R1 + R3 lite shipped previous Ω-cycle)
inputs.recipient: tool/bridge_health.sh (150 lines, no per-entry hash defense, no --strict mode)
inputs.asymmetry: tool/roadmap_dod_bridge.hexa exists but is not in 16-bridge registry → out of scope (matches existing bridge_health.sh BRIDGES list)

constraint_compliance.raw_66_reason_fix: TAMPERED line emits reason=bridge_sha256_mismatch + fix=audit_git_log_or_refresh_baseline + declared/live hashes; FAIL line emits reason=selftest_failed + fix=run_verbose_then_check_design
constraint_compliance.raw_71_report_only: no auto-mutation of bridges; STRICT mode warns (not fails) when baseline missing
constraint_compliance.raw_73_admissibility: all classification deltas backed by real bash exec results captured below — no fabrication
constraint_compliance.raw_77_audit_append_only: additive patch — no existing logic deleted, only new STRICT branch + per-bridge SHA check + TAMPERED counter; baseline TSV created additively at state/bridge_sha256.tsv
constraint_compliance.raw_80_sentinel: __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ extended with tampered=K field; __HEALTH_CHECK_ALL__ extended with tampered=Ft+Bt field; backward compatible parsers (older sed scripts ignore extra fields)

axes_surfaced.name: baseline_storage_strategy
options_considered: inline_hardcoded_in_script (rejected: forces script edit per bridge change, conflates state with code)
separate_TSV_state_bridge_sha256_tsv (chosen: mirrors falsifier_registry.sha256 precedent, content-addressed, single source of truth)
axes_surfaced.rationale: Same shape as falsifier_registry.sha256 (single-line baseline) but per-entry granularity needed because bridges are 16 independent files vs falsifiers being one JSONL registry

axes_surfaced.name: phase_1_baseline_generation
axes_surfaced.method: python3 hashlib.sha256 over raw bytes of each .hexa, truncated to 16 hex chars (matches falsifier sha256_16 helper)
axes_surfaced.git_visibility: ignored by .gitignore line 266 (state/*) — same precedent as state/falsifier_registry.sha256

axes_surfaced.name: phase_2_bridge_health_sh_patch
additions: sha256_file_16() helper (shasum BSD → sha256sum GNU fallback)
declared_sha_for() awk lookup over baseline TSV
TAMPERED=0 counter init + per-bridge SHA check before selftest invocation
STATUS=TAMPERED branch with raw 66 reason+fix trailer + declared/live hash dump
OFFLINE-FALLBACK ec≠0 branch (degraded but ran) with separate counter increment
tampered field in JSONL line + summary + sentinel

axes_surfaced.name: HIT_vs_ERROR_bridge_disambiguation
axes_surfaced.key_difference: Bridges have heterogeneous sentinel formats (__NAME_BRIDGE__ PASS vs [name_bridge selftest] OK) — both accepted. Falsifier had one sentinel type. Bridges also have legitimate offline-fallback path (network unreachable + cached fallback engaged) which is degraded-not-failed; falsifiers don't have that mode.
axes_surfaced.non_trivial_heuristic: Per raw 73, ec≠0 alone does not auto-classify — must check OUT for fallback|offline|no.?fetch substring before reclassifying as OFFLINE-FALLBACK. Pure ec≠0 with no fallback signal stays FAIL.

axes_surfaced.name: wrapper_aggregator_tampered_surfacing
additions: raw 66 fix trailer: 'R1 tamper detected — audit git log of mutated files; refresh baseline if intended' (only when TAMP_TOTAL>0)

axes_surfaced.name: verification_pre_mutation
axes_surfaced.sentinel: __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ PASS total=16 pass=16 fail=0 tampered=0 duration_ms=33000

axes_surfaced.name: verification_during_codata_tamper
axes_surfaced.per_bridge_line: codata        TAMPERED   ec=N/A  reason=bridge_sha256_mismatch  fix=audit_git_log_or_refresh_baseline  declared=3f9992f3d5893c59  live=3a85a2c5c64565d5
axes_surfaced.sentinel: __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ FAIL total=16 pass=15 fail=1 tampered=1 duration_ms=33000
axes_surfaced.summary_line: total=16  pass=15  fail=1  offline_fallback=3  tampered=1  wall=33000ms
axes_surfaced.outcome: 15/16 PASS + 1 TAMPERED (codata) — selftest skipped after SHA mismatch (raw 71 fail-fast on tamper, no risk of running mutated body)

axes_surfaced.name: verification_post_restore
axes_surfaced.sentinel: __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ PASS total=16 pass=16 fail=0 tampered=0 duration_ms=34000
axes_surfaced.outcome: 16/16 PASS, tampered=0 — fixpoint reached, no residue from mutation

axes_surfaced.name: health_check_all_wrapper_smoke
axes_surfaced.human_line: F: 89/91 CLEAN | B: 16/16 PASS | tampered=0 (F=0 B=0) | S: status_all_ok
axes_surfaced.note: FAIL verdict is from pre-existing falsifier HITs (F: 89/91 not 91/91) — orthogonal to this Ω-cycle. Bridge subcheck cleanly reports 16/16 + tampered=0. Aggregator tampered field successfully surfaces both sub-counts.

tier_1_promotion_witnesses.name: bridge_sha256.tsv_created
tier_1_promotion_witnesses.evidence: /Users/ghost/core/nexus/state/bridge_sha256.tsv — 20 lines (4 header + 16 data), 1499 bytes, all 16 bridge hashes pinned

tier_1_promotion_witnesses.name: bridge_health.sh_R1_patched
tier_1_promotion_witnesses.evidence: tool/bridge_health.sh: 150→215 lines, +65 lines, TAMPERED counter + --strict flag + sha256_file_16 helper + declared_sha_for awk lookup + per-bridge runtime SHA check before selftest. Mutation test: codata tamper → STATUS=TAMPERED with declared=3f9992f3d5893c59 live=3a85a2c5c64565d5

tier_1_promotion_witnesses.name: health_check_all.sh_aggregator_tampered_surfacing
tier_1_promotion_witnesses.evidence: tool/health_check_all.sh: 74→80 lines, +6 lines, F_TAMP+B_TAMP parsed and aggregated; sentinel extended with tampered=N field; human line shows tampered=0 (F=0 B=0) breakdown

tier_1_promotion_witnesses.name: fixpoint_marker
tier_1_promotion_witnesses.evidence: Pre-mutation 16/16 PASS tampered=0 → during-mutation 15/16 PASS + 1 TAMPERED (codata flagged with raw 66 trailer) → post-restore 16/16 PASS tampered=0. codata_bridge.hexa byte-identical to baseline post-restore (sha 3f9992f3d5893c59 matches). git status confirms only tool/bridge_health.sh + tool/health_check_all.sh modified; codata_bridge.hexa untouched.

non_witnessed_gaps.description: No companion tool/bridge_sha256_pin.py created — the TSV regeneration is currently ad-hoc python3 inline. If a bridge gets a legitimate edit, operator must manually rerun the inline snippet from this witness. Recommend follow-up Ω-cycle to ship tool/bridge_sha256_pin.py mirroring how falsifier baselines are refreshed.

tool/roadmap_dod_bridge.hexa exists in tool/ but is not in the 16-bridge BRIDGES list of bridge_health.sh — it falls outside both selftest invocation and SHA pinning. Either register as 17th bridge or document as out-of-scope helper. Inherited from upstream — not in this cycle's scope.

--strict flag accepted but only warns when baseline TSV is missing. Mismatch detection is always on (TAMPERED auto-flips sentinel to FAIL regardless of --strict). The flag is currently a no-op for the runtime path; reserved for future R3-style policy escalation.

Baseline TSV itself is unsigned — an attacker who can rewrite a bridge .hexa can also rewrite the TSV. Mitigation requires an out-of-band signature (gpg / sigstore) or git-tracked baseline (currently .gitignore'd by state/* pattern, mirroring falsifier precedent). Defer to post-cycle policy decision.

fixpoint_marker: REACHED — 16/16 PASS tampered=0 baseline before mutation, 15/16 + 1 tampered during codata mutation (correctly detected with raw 66 trailer), 16/16 PASS tampered=0 after restore. Three-way mutation cycle confirms detector is sound (no false negatives) and reversible (no residue). codata_bridge.hexa byte-identical pre/post (sha 3f9992f3d5893c59).

modified: tool/bridge_health.sh (+65 lines: --strict flag, sha256_file_16, declared_sha_for, TAMPERED counter, per-bridge SHA check, OFFLINE-FALLBACK ec≠0 branch, sentinel tampered=N field)
tool/health_check_all.sh (+6 lines: F_TAMP/B_TAMP parse, TAMP_TOTAL aggregate, sentinel tampered=N field, raw 66 fix trailer when tampered>0)
created: state/bridge_sha256.tsv (16 bridge hashes, 4 header lines, .gitignore-d per state/* precedent)
test_mutations_undone: tool/codata_bridge.hexa: tampered then restored byte-identical (sha 3f9992f3d5893c59 matches baseline; git status clean for this file)

raw_66_trailers_emitted: Summary line: 'fix: audit git log of mutated bridge(s); if intended, refresh state/bridge_sha256.tsv' (when tampered>0)
Wrapper: 'fix: R1 tamper detected — audit git log of mutated files; refresh baseline if intended' (when TAMP_TOTAL>0)

compliance_artifacts.raw_80_sentinel_evolution: __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ <P|F> total=N pass=P fail=F duration_ms=T → __BRIDGE_HEALTH__ <P|F> total=N pass=P fail=F tampered=K duration_ms=T (backward compatible: legacy sed parsers ignore unknown fields)
